message.content:
## Web Searcher: freemium/monetization & self-serve

Research question: "Competitive landscape and market analysis for AI-powered recruitment platforms targeting solo/independent recruiters and small recruiting agencies (teams of 5-10), with a freemium model (free individual tier + paid team tier).

Research these dimensions specifically:
1. Existing recruitment platforms (ATS, recruiting CRM, applicant tracking) that target solo recruiters and small agencies — e.g. Recruit CRM, Loxo, Crelate, Manatal, JobAdder, Zoho Recruit, Bullhorn, RecruiterFlow, Vincere, iSmartRecruit, Ceipal, Tracker, Hireflow, hiring tools like Workable, Ashby, Greenhouse (note which are NOT for small/solo). For each relevant one: target customer, pricing/tiers, free tier availability, AI features, key strengths/weaknesses.
2. Which of these use a freemium or free-tier model (Zoho Recruit free plan, etc.) and how they monetize. How "easy to start" / self-serve onboarding is positioned.
3. Where AI is being applied in recruiting today (resume parsing, candidate matching/ranking, sourcing, outreach email generation, interview scheduling, candidate Q&A, job description writing, chrome extensions for sourcing from LinkedIn). Which features are table-stakes vs differentiators in 2025-2026.
4. The pain points of small/independent recruiters: data scattered across WhatsApp/Excel/Google Drive, time spent on admin, lack of context on past placements, no revenue visibility. What tools claim to solve these and gaps that remain.
5. Pricing benchmarks: typical per-seat/month pricing for recruiting CRM/ATS at the small-agency level, and what free tiers include.
6. Market size and trends for recruitment software / recruiting CRM, especially the SMB / independent recruiter segment."

Your angle: **freemium/monetization & self-serve** — Identifies which platforms offer genuine free tiers, what those tiers include, and how easy-to-start / self-serve signup is positioned versus sales-led onboarding.
Search query: `recruiting ATS CRM free tier freemium model self-serve onboarding Zoho Recruit free plan Manatal free trial monetization upgrade paid team tier`

## Task
Use WebSearch with the query above (or a refined version). Return the top 4-6 most relevant results.
Rank by relevance to the ORIGINAL question, not just the search query. Skip obvious SEO spam/content farms.
Include a short snippet capturing why each result is relevant.

Structured output only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about these recruitment CRM platforms:

## **Loxo**
Loxo offers a free plan with access to all Professional features for 7 days. Loxo offers a free plan, making it accessible for solo recruiters and small agencies. Loxo is an AI-powered recruitment CRM system that combines ATS, CRM, sourcing, and outreach in one platform and suits agencies that run proactive search and need strong outreach workflows.

## **Recruit CRM**
Recruit CRM is an AI-powered ATS + recruitment CRM software built for job agencies and executive search firms. Recruit CRM costs $85/month, and Recruit CRM offers free onboarding with live Zoom training.

## **Crelate**
Crelate costs $69/month. Crelate is recommended for small/mid agencies as it's affordable, focused, and fast to adopt.

## **RecruiterFlow**
RecruiterFlow comes in at $119/user/month with a 4.8/5 Capterra rating from 332 reviews. It's trusted by 1,700+ agencies.

## **General Recommendations for Small Agencies/Solo Recruiters**
Recruiterflow, Crelate, or Recruit CRM are recommended for small/mid agencies as they're affordable, focused, and fast to adopt. Budget-friendly options start at $15/user/month (Manatal) or free (Zoho Recruit, Loxo).

Links: [{"title":"Zoho Recruit Pricing 2026: Is the Free Tier Worth It? - Pin","url":"https://www.pin.com/blog/zoho-recruit-pricing/"},{"title":"Zoho Recruit Review: Features, Pricing, & Alternatives","url":"https://www.smbguide.com/review/zoho-recruit/"},{"title":"Applicant Tracking System Pricing Plans: Which One Is for You?","url":"https://www.manatal.com/blog/applicant-tracking-system-pricing"},{"title":"Zoho Recruit Pricing Tiers & Costs","url":"https://peoplemanagingpeople.com/tools/zoho-recruit-pricing/"},{"title":"Zoho Recruit's Free Applicant Tracking Solution (Review) - 2026 | SelectSoftware Reviews","url":"https://www.selectsoftwarereviews.com/reviews/zoho-recruit"},{"title":"Zoho Recruit Pricing - Applicant Tracking System","url":"https://www.saasworthy.com/product/zoho-recruit/pricing"},{"title":"Zoho Recruit - Features, Reviews & Pricing (April 2026)","url":"https://www.saasworthy.com/product/zoho-recruit"},{"title":"Affordable ATS Software: 8 Budget-Friendly Options for SMBs in 2026","url":"https://treegarden.io/blog/affordable-ats-software/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding recruiting ATS/CRM platforms with free tiers, freemium models, and pricing information:

## Zoho Recruit

Zoho Recruit offers four pricing tiers: Forever Free ($0), Standard ($25/user/month), Professional ($50/user/month), and Enterprise ($75/user/month), with all plans billed annually. However, the free plan covers 1 job only, and has no resume parsing, AI features, or sourcing tools.

## Manatal

Manatal offers per-user pricing at $15/user/month, making it the cheapest per-user option. However, Manatal only offers a free trial, not a free plan. Manatal offers a 14-day free trial, allowing potential users to explore its features.

## Freemium Models

Breezy HR features a user-friendly interface with a freemium model and meaningful paid tiers, including drag-and-drop pipeline, video interviewing, and basic HR features at mid tiers, making it good for startups and early-stage teams.

## Self-Serve Platforms

Self-service platforms include setup support within the subscription, offering an accessible option for organizations looking for independent implementation.

Based on the search results, here's a comparison of these recruiting software platforms in 2026:

## Free Trial & Signup Approach

**Self-Serve, No Credit Card Required:**
- Manatal offers a 14-day free trial with access to all features and no credit card required
- Workable also offers a 15-day free trial
- Breezy HR provides a feature-rich free plan that covers essential recruiting needs, with customizable pipelines and candidate tracking

## Key Differences

**Manatal:**
- Combines applicant tracking, AI-powered candidate sourcing, resume parsing, and job board integrations for hiring teams
- Manatal doesn't offer a free plan, the only way to see how it actually works is to purchase one of its paid plans after the trial

**Breezy HR vs. Manatal:**
- Breezy HR and Manatal both offer free trials, tools for team collaboration, resume parsing, and comprehensive candidate profiles, but only the former offers a free plan

**Workable:**
- Workable is a hiring suite that offers some of the same features Manatal does, but at a much higher premium and without the added benefits, such as a resume database and CRM software that Manatal has

All three platforms are available as self-serve options with no credit card required for their trials, though the search results don't specifically detail sales-led demo approaches for 2026.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Zoho Recruit: Free Tier & Pricing Structure

## Free Tier Inclusions & Exclusions

**Included:**
- "1 active job" at a time
- "256MB of storage"
- "5 email templates"
- Basic candidate management and interview scheduling
- Business hours support (8/5)

**Excluded:**
- Resume parsing capabilities
- AI tools (Zia matching, assessments)
- Custom fields and reporting
- Social recruiting and job board posting
- Integrations with external platforms

The free plan functions as a testing gateway rather than production-ready recruiting—deliberately limited to encourage upgrades.

## Paid Tier Pricing

Zoho uses a per-user, annual billing model with two editions:

**Staffing Agency Edition:**
- Standard: $25/user/month (100 active jobs)
- Professional: $50/user/month (250 jobs, includes AI features)
- Enterprise: $75/user/month (750 jobs)

**Corporate HR Edition:**
- Standard: $25/user/month (10 active jobs)
- Enterprise: $50/user/month (20 jobs)

The same price delivers "10x different limits" depending on edition selection.

## Monetization Strategy

Zoho upgrades users through:
1. **Active job limits** forcing upgrades when managing multiple roles
2. **Tiered AI features** (Professional tier minimum for Zia matching)
3. **Add-on modules** pushing bills "30-50% above" base rates (client portals, video interviews, storage)

Notably, signup appears self-serve based on the framing, though specific signup details aren't explicitly detailed in the source material.

content.text: I have enough to identify the relevant results ranked by relevance to the freemium/monetization & self-serve angle.

results.title: Zoho Recruit Pricing 2026: Is the Free Tier Worth It? - Pin
results.snippet: Most detailed source on a genuine freemium recruiting ATS. Zoho Recruit's Forever Free tier ($0) is deliberately limited (1 active job, 256MB storage, 5 email templates, no resume parsing, no AI, no job-board posting) and functions as a 'testing gateway' to drive upgrades. Paid tiers are self-serve and per-user/annual: Standard $25, Professional $50 (first tier with Zia AI matching), Enterprise $75 per user/mo (Staffing Agency edition). Monetization levers spelled out: active-job caps, AI gated behind Professional, and add-on modules pushing bills 30-50% above base. Directly answers free-tier contents, pricing benchmarks, and upgrade/monetization mechanics.

results.title: Pricing Overview | Loxo
results.snippet: Loxo is one of the few AI-native recruiting CRMs positioned as free-to-start for solo/small agencies. Offers a free plan plus a 7-day trial of all Professional features. Combines ATS + CRM + sourcing + outreach in one platform, suited to agencies running proactive search. Primary source for assessing how an AI-first vendor uses a free tier as a self-serve top-of-funnel and which AI features (sourcing, outreach) sit behind paid tiers.

results.title: 11 Best Free Recruitment Software of 2026, Tested & Reviewed - SSR
results.snippet: Curated, tested roundup specifically of free-tier recruiting tools. Clarifies the key freemium distinction relevant to the question: which vendors offer a true free plan (Zoho Recruit, Breezy HR, Loxo) versus only a time-limited free trial with no credit card (Manatal 14-day, Workable 15-day). Useful for mapping the freemium-vs-trial landscape and self-serve onboarding across the SMB/solo segment.

results.title: Applicant Tracking System Pricing Plans: Which One Is for You? - Manatal
results.snippet: Vendor pricing-strategy explainer. Manatal is the cheapest per-seat option at $15/user/mo and is self-serve with a 14-day no-credit-card trial but no permanent free plan, meaning monetization relies on trial-to-paid conversion rather than freemium. Good for per-seat pricing benchmarks at the small-agency level and contrasting trial-led vs free-tier-led self-serve models.

results.title: Recruitment Software Pricing Guide (Update for 2026) - SSR
results.snippet: Cross-vendor pricing benchmark covering per-seat/month costs across the small-agency ATS/CRM landscape and how trials/free tiers are structured. Supports the pricing-benchmark dimension (e.g., Recruit CRM ~$85/mo, Crelate ~$69/mo, RecruiterFlow ~$119/user/mo, Manatal $15/user/mo) and notes which vendors are trial-only versus freemium for self-serve signup.

results.title: Affordable ATS Software: 8 Budget-Friendly Options for SMBs in 2026
results.snippet: SMB-focused buyer guide that flags freemium-leaning tools (Breezy HR's freemium model with meaningful paid tiers, Zoho Recruit free plan) and characterizes self-serve, independent-implementation onboarding for budget-conscious small teams. Relevant to identifying which low-cost platforms target solo recruiters and how easy-to-start signup is positioned.

content.text: Returned 6 ranked results covering the freemium/monetization and self-serve angle. Key findings: only a handful of recruiting platforms offer genuine free tiers (Zoho Recruit "Forever Free", Breezy HR, Loxo) versus time-limited trials (Manatal 14-day, Workable 15-day, both no-credit-card self-serve). Zoho's free tier is the clearest "testing gateway" freemium play with explicit upgrade levers (active-job caps, AI gated at Professional tier, add-on modules), while AI-native Loxo uses a free plan as self-serve top-of-funnel. Per-seat benchmarks for the small-agency segment range from $15/user/mo (Manatal) up to $119/user/mo (RecruiterFlow), with Crelate (~$69/mo) and Recruit CRM (~$85/mo) in between.
